scope: Every completed post-v2, post-v2.1, and QCSA v2.3 result that was a promotion recommendation, an accepted prior transition, or plausibly supports a narrower bounded claim.

candidates.candidate_claim: Sixteen model-generated plan/code runs provide bounded local comparative evidence for authority, effect observation, receipt/path reconciliation, residuals, rollback, quarantine, and measured cost, while zero governed releases and two failed rollbacks block broader promotion.
candidates.comparator: The preregistered matched baseline, ablation, challenger, or no-update route named by the frozen campaign and transition record.
candidates.cohort: eight disposable local tasks; one small local coder model; exact retained result denominator only.
candidates.cost: Campaign-recorded latency, token/tool, review, rollback, state, or resource fields are retained; unmeasured production cost is not inferred.
candidates.independence: local maintainer-agent review only; no external human review requested or claimed
candidates.transfer: No population, production, institutional, or open-world transfer is established beyond the exact local workload.
validity_limits: no deployed authorization, sandbox, verifier, rollback, or release service
counterevidence: governed route released zero useful candidates
two governed rollbacks remained inexact and quarantined
four governed residuals were discovered and two remained open
the prevalidation pilot was preserved as non-evidentiary after protocol defects
residuals: deployed or externally reviewable governed-work traces
broader natural workloads and models
independent verifier-quality assessment
production cost, throughput, rollback, and residual evidence
permitted_changes: Preserve the bounded result and its negative evidence in the owning chapter/ledger.
Clarify only the exact local mechanism or blocker; do not move a chapter-core support state.
candidates.prohibited_inference: deployed or externally reviewable governed-work traces; broader natural workloads and models; independent verifier-quality assessment; production cost, throughput, rollback, and residual evidence

candidates.candidate_claim: The active-question mechanism improves held-out decisions in this exact corpus.
candidates.comparator: Frozen QCSA baselines or component ablations under the exact preregistered corpus, labels, resource accounting, and observer implementation.
candidates.cohort: 60 held-out deterministic templates repeated across three seeds (180 records), or the exact separately named bounded reference trace.
candidates.cost: QCSA used 729 total operations (4.05 mean) and a 1.913386 matched operation ratio; proxy latency, verifier work, questions, and human burden remain visible.
candidates.independence: Evaluator/observer is separately implemented but internal to the same project; no external replication or human review occurred.
candidates.transfer: Template-transparent synthetic fixtures and local temporary-file effects do not establish natural-language, learned-model, distributed, or production transfer.
validity_limits: Question-policy cost is additionally confounded by the frozen ablation verifier-cost bookkeeping; no general claim against clarification is licensed.
counterevidence: Removing active questions left object and task-decision accuracy at 1.000; the full candidate asked questions on 0.033333 of records and gained no decision accuracy.
Question-policy cost is additionally confounded by the frozen ablation verifier-cost bookkeeping; no general claim against clarification is licensed.
residuals: Ceiling-easy labels, internal authorship, deterministic templates, proxy cost, and absent natural prevalence remain material.
permitted_changes: Record the exact bounded non-core result and its disposition in the non-core evidence ledger.
Revise owning prose only to state the exact mechanism result, counterevidence, cost, and transfer boundary; keep every chapter core at argument.
candidates.prohibited_inference: No natural-task, learned-model, open-world, production, safety, privacy, security, external-independence, AGI, ASI, or chapter-core support inference is permitted.

candidates.cohort: 60 held-out deterministic templates repeated across three seeds (180 records), or the exact separately named bounded reference trace.
candidates.cost: QCSA used 729 total operations (4.05 mean) and a 1.913386 matched operation ratio; proxy latency, verifier work, questions, and human burden remain visible.
candidates.independence: Evaluator/observer is separately implemented but internal to the same project; no external replication or human review occurred.
candidates.transfer: Template-transparent synthetic fixtures and local temporary-file effects do not establish natural-language, learned-model, distributed, or production transfer.
validity_limits: Brier targets task-decision correctness, not object correctness; many task labels were ceiling-easy.
counterevidence: QCSA Brier score was 0.082026 versus 0.298500 for the selected best baseline, with selective risk 0.000000 for both, so the frozen calibration gate passed.
Brier targets task-decision correctness, not object correctness; many task labels were ceiling-easy.
residuals: Ceiling-easy labels, internal authorship, deterministic templates, proxy cost, and absent natural prevalence remain material.
permitted_changes: Record the exact bounded non-core result and its disposition in the non-core evidence ledger.
Revise owning prose only to state the exact mechanism result, counterevidence, cost, and transfer boundary; keep every chapter core at argument.
candidates.prohibited_inference: No natural-task, learned-model, open-world, production, safety, privacy, security, external-independence, AGI, ASI, or chapter-core support inference is permitted.
